title: Signal Phishing Attack: Digital Evidence Points to Russia
content: For weeks, a wave of attacks has been targeting Signal users in Germany and abroad. Among those targeted are security officials, politicians, and journalists. A CORRECTIV investigation found digital evidence pointing to Russian involvement in the campaign – and a connection to previous attacks in Ukraine and Moldova.
creator: Max Bernhard

title: Housing Cost: One in Seven Municipalities in Europe Unaffordable for Nurses
content: Data analysis by CORRECTIV.Europe reveals: In these European locations, even a mid-level salary like a nurse's is not enough to afford a small apartment.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: The New German War Machine
content: Germany’s transformation into a military power is in full swing: confidential government documents reveal plans to channel billions into ammunition and controversial weapons systems. Is the country prepared for the consequences?
creator: Till Eckert

title: Learning to Backslide: How Autocrats Share Money, Methods, and Models
content: Democracy is not dying by sudden coup – it is being dismantled through the law itself, using a shared playbook refined across continents.
creator: Team CORRECTIV.Exile

title: Azerbaijan: The Price of Victory and the Silence of Dissent
content:
In this article series, we take a second look at current events in countries that often remain only briefly spotlighted in German reporting. Together with local experts, we ask: What political and social developments lie behind the current events that we see in the news? What does this mean for democracy and media freedom? With our exile expertise, we want to reveal global connections and understand what we can learn from this for free, democratic coexistence.
In this episode, Azerbaijani journalist Fatima Karimova writes about the repression of media workers in her homeland and why the European Union repeatedly turns a blind eye to it.
creator: Viera Zuborova

title: Russia declares CORRECTIV an “undesirable organization”
content: The Russian Prosecutor General's Office has designated CORRECTIV as an “undesirable foreign organization.” With this move, the Russian state is placing our journalistic work under blanket threat of punishment—while simultaneously demonstrating that our reporting is effective. Independent investigations are to be made impossible in Russia.
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: Despite sanctions: Putin‘s war waged with European machinery
content: Without machines from abroad, Russia’s weapons factories would grind to a halt. This analysis of Russian import data shows German and European companies supplied goods with potential dual-use applications over 28,000 times in the lead-up to the invasion. How this has impacted the war is often overlooked.
creator: CORRECTIV

title: “Every minute, a building in Europe is destroyed”
content: The EU wants millions of old buildings to be renovated. But frequently the opposite happens: existing houses are demolished and replaced by new builds. Exactly how many buildings are being torn down remains a blind spot. CORRECTIV wants to close this gap in the data.
creator: Marius Münstermann

title: How Much European Real Estate is Owned by Russians? The EU has no Idea
content: Despite sweeping sanctions, Russian nationals can still legally purchase property in the EU. Several member states view this as a major security risk. Our investigation reveals how an EU-wide ban collapsed due to the resistance of a single country.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: Where Europe is Dumping its Problematic Waste
content: In the middle of Europe, a contaminated legacy of the Soviet nuclear program has turned into a lucrative opportunity for the waste industry. Our investigation reveals that companies continue to dump millions of tons of old tyres, shoes, and even hazardous waste.
creator: Marius Münstermann

title: The Man Suing Trump
content: A Conversation with Ben Wizner, US Civil Rights Attorney
creator: Jean Peters

title: Demolition Atlas Europe launched: CORRECTIV investigates demolitions across Europe
content: Every year, hundreds of thousands of buildings are demolished across Europe. In cooperation with international media, we uncover the ecological, social and cultural consequences.
creator: Marius Münstermann

title: Where Travel Platforms like Airbnb have Transformed Europe’s Tourism
content: Short-term rentals through online platforms are surging – far outpacing traditional tourism. Using local-level data, CORRECTIV.Europe reveals for the first time where EU residents are confronted with especially high numbers of visitors.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: Eleven football players report more than a hundred cases of intimate touching by a German player consultant
content: Research by CORRECTIV shows: The often underage young footballers, some of whom now play professional football, are said to have been treated naked in the counsellor's offices under the pretext of sports medicine treatment.
creator: Jonathan Sachse

title: The Taliban’s Algorithmic Emirate: How bots can reinforce and shape a political system
content: How the Taliban use fake identities and bot accounts to simulate support – and exploit the digital space for their propaganda. An investigation into targeted manipulation, virtual control, and the threat to open societies.
creator: Team CORRECTIV.Exile

title: “It’s difficult to explain that you freeze to death at 5 degrees Celsius”
content: An estimated 47 Million people in Europe cannot afford a warm home in winter, according to a calculation from CORRECTIV.Europe. Reports from local journalists in our network show the dimension of the problem in several countries.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: “I’m always cold”
content: 47 million people in Europe cannot afford to heat their homes in winter, according to an analysis by CORRECTIV.Europe. Millions are also affected in Germany. Experts warn this may lead to social conflict.
creator: Mohamed Anwar

title: EU states not prepared for new carbon pricing
content: Carbon pricing for transport and heating fuels is to be introduced across the EU from 2027. But several countries are now questioning the rapid phase-out of fossil fuels – which could mean even higher costs in the long term. “We are sleepwalking towards a political disaster,” social democratic MEP Thomas Pellerin-Carlin warns.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: Influence operation exposed: How Russia meddles in Germany’s election campaign
content: A CORRECTIV investigation has uncovered that a Russian disinformation campaign established around 100 fake websites ahead of Germany’s general election. Their purpose: to influence the election campaign due to take place in February. In several cases, these sites have already been used to discredit German politicians.
creator: Sarah Thust

title: Doppelganger: CORRECTIV investigations bring Russian propaganda campaign to a halt
content: The spread of fake websites by the Russian disinformation campaign Doppelganger has largely been stopped. The people behind the campaign had been using a Ukrainian service provider – which quickly blocked the Russian customer after a CORRECTIV inquiry. Doppelganger’s digital footprints meanwhile lead directly to the Ministry of Defense in Moscow.
creator: Sarah Thust

title: Europe On Fire: How Rising Temperatures Are Threatening Energy Supply
content: Hotter summers pose a serious health risk. They also threaten the stability of energy supplies throughout Europe. CORRECTIV.Europe shows on a detailed map which regions are affected the most.
creator: Frida Thurm

title: CORRECTIV.Europe with new leadership: Joanna Krawczyk becomes Managing Director, Frida Thurm strengthens the editorial team
content: The public interest non-profit media house CORRECTIV is advancing the restructuring of its European department CORRECTIV.Europe with international expertise: Joanna Krawczyk takes over as Managing Director of CORRECTIV.Europe as of October 1, 2024. With her extensive experience in the European media landscape and her commitment to independent journalism, she will drive the strategic development and execution of CORRECTIV.Europe forward.
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: CORRECTIV.Europe restructures, editor-in-chief Olaya Argüeso Pérez leaves CORRECTIV
content: The media house CORRECTIV is restructuring one of its core projects: CORRECTIV.Europe is to become an even stronger central hub for Europe's local editors, journalists and communities. As part of the restructuring of CORRECTIV.Europe, Olaya Argüeso Pérez is leaving CORRECTIV as Editor-in-Chief.
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: Inside Doppelganger – How Russia uses EU companies for its propaganda
content: For two years, Russia has been spreading its propaganda using cloned websites of Western media outlets. The campaign known as "Doppelganger" hasn’t been stopped yet – despite being sanctioned by the EU and US. A CORRECTIV investigation found that European companies are part of the internet infrastructure that keeps the campaign running.
creator: Max Bernhard

title: In the Glass Palace – Abuse of Power and Sexual Harassment in the EU Parliament
content: Just weeks before the EU elections, a contentious issue is forcing its way onto the agenda behind the scenes at the European Parliament. Sexual assault and bullying within Parliament itself. Several dozen women and men have reported cases of unwanted touching, emotional abuse and physical violence to CORRECTIV and the German newspaper Stern—mostly by MEPs.
creator: Gabriela Keller

title: Hacks and Propaganda: Meet the Two Brothers Bringing Russia’s Cyber War to Europe
content: Since its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Russia has stepped up disinformation and hacking campaigns against the West. A CORRECTIV investigation shows how two brothers from Moldova provide the technology needed for these attacks.
creator: Max Bernhard

title: Don’t Bother Asking AI About the EU Elections: How Chatbots Fail When It Comes to Politics
content: We asked three of the best-known AI chatbots multiple questions on the upcoming EU elections and international politics. Our experiment shows: Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT fail when it comes to answering political questions. They make false statements about candidates or fabricate sources. Depending on the language, the bots also provide very different answers.
creator: Viktor Marinov

title: The EU’s Long Road Towards Sustainable Agriculture
content: The EU has committed itself to become carbon-neutral by 2050. Farming plays a key role in that goal. However, the sector’s lobbying efforts have allowed large players to avoid stricter environmental controls.
creator: Mohamed Anwar

title: The Green Gas Lie
content: German gas providers‘ claims about their “climate-neutral” natural gas are little more than greenwashing, CORRECTIV has revealed. The gas is linked to the deforestation of supposedly protected forests and the expansion of gas-fired power stations. We expose an industry deceiving hundreds of thousands of customers and fuelling the climate crisis.
creator: Mohamed Anwar

title: Secret plan against Germany
content: It was the meeting that nobody was ever meant to find out about. Back in November, high-ranking politicians from Germany’s far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, neo-Nazis, and sympathetic businesspeople gathered in a hotel near Potsdam. Their agenda? Nothing less than the fine tuning of a plan for the forced deportations of millions of people currently living in Germany.
creator: Valentin Zick

title: How CORRECTIV’s journalism made a difference this year
content: The German government launches an investigation, a court takes the ex-pope to court and a sanction is imposed: CORRECTIV's research once again had direct impacts in 2023. We show what has changed for the better.
creator: Justus von Daniels

title: CORRECTIV is working on a new AI tool
content: In the JournalismAI Fellowship, CORRECTIV developed the prototype for a new AI application within an international team of editors and developers.
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: German rifles for Russia
content: Since the beginning of Russia's war in Ukraine more than 7000 rifles and more than 8 million shots of ammunition made by Western manufacturers made their way to Russia. Some of them are being used in the front lines. German manufacturers are affected, too – caused by sanction loopholes.
creator: Anette Dowideit

title: Industrial air pollution costs Europe 265 billion euros in one year
content: Europe's coal-fired power plants poison our air like no other industry. Giants such as PGE, RWE or Vattenfall emit tons of pollutants yearly, with harsh consequences for our health and the environment. The damage cost us billions of euros.
creator: Olaya Argüeso Pérez

title: Alternative for Russia: How the AfD is systematically turning towards Russia
content: The German far-right political party Alternative for Germany (Alternative für Deutschland) has set its sights on Russia. Their election manifesto barely mentions the West or NATO. Provocative trips, aggressive political speeches and pro-Russian motions in the Bundestag (German parliament) all reveal a party whose foreign policy is pandering to Moscow.
creator: Marcus Bensmann

title: Deforestation: German development bank is financing environmental destruction
content: Germany's national development bank DEG is funding dubious projects in the Global South. CORRECTIV and El Surtidor have been reporting on the deforestation crisis in Paraguay which is co-financed by the DEG. The German government is doing nothing about it.
creator: Gesa Steeger

title: Job posting: Freelance Developer Frontend / Backend
content: part time, freelance for 2 month, Berlin/Essen or remote
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: Politicians of Germany’s far-right AfD are benefitting from a pro-Russian propaganda campaign. Here is how.
content: A network of fake accounts on Facebook is agitating against Ukraine and has been sharing fake pro-Russian articles and websites for months. Politicians of the far-right party Alternative für Deutschland seem to also benefit: the network ran ads to promote content by the party’s politicians.
creator: Max Bernhard

title: Facebook silent as pro-Kremlin disinformation campaign rages on
content: Despite Facebook’s supposed takedown last year, a pro-Russian disinformation campaign continues to be active on the social-media platform. It is promoting spoofed news and government websites, as well as content by politicians of Germany’s far-right AfD party.
creator: Max Bernhard

title: Dirty pensions: Climate activists know nothing about their investments in gas and oil
content: Anyone who puts their money into pension funds risks profiting from climate-damaging corporations in Germany as well - unknowingly. An international and local investigation led by CORRECTIV reveals connections for the first time.
creator: CORRECTIV

title: Chinese fraudsters systematically smuggle clothing into the EU
content: Chinese fraudsters smuggling masses of textiles into Europe are evading import fees. CORRECTIV research shows: The scam is costing the EU billions of euros. Customs cannot keep up with controls and member states are looking the other way.
creator: Till Eckert

title: Call for applications for illustrators – Graphic Novel on Community Investigation
content: The Seek Initiative in collaboration with CORRECTIV is looking for an illustrator for a graphic novel on the topic of community investigation. The illustrator will work closely with the team composed of the author, an illustrations coordinator at CORRECTIV and a point person at the Seek Initiative.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Vodka, Scholz and Gazprom
content:
Vodka, Scholz and Gazprom This is how young lobbyists celebrated good business with Russia – and fueled gas dependency. They really let their hair down: white rabbits, dirndls and Stalin doubles all made appearances during the festivities. Sponsored by a host of companies from Gazprom to Porsche, a new generation of German-Russian business lobbyists celebrated. […]

The post Vodka, Scholz and Gazprom appeared first on correctiv.org.
creator: Friedrich Breitschuh

title: Devastating Supply Chains
content: The German federal government is lobbying in Brussels for a restriction of the liability rules in the EU Supply Chain Act – seemingly under pressure from the Free Democratic Party (FDP) and industry. This has been revealed by investigations by CORRECTIV and Frontal. The example of natural rubber shows how demand in Europe drives deforestation and displacement in Africa.
creator: Nina Bender

title: Charcoal: Somalia’s burning issue
content: In Somalia, terrorists and parts of the state profit from the charcoal trade, according to those involved – a trade which is harmful to both nature and the population
creator: Nina Bender

title: The Amazon machine
content: Amazon sends millions of parcels before Christmas in Germany. Powering the logistics giant is a system built on pressure and surveillance.
creator: Nina Bender

title: Forged government documents and fake news sites: Russian disinformation campaign targets Germany
content: A network of fake news sites has been flooding Europe with disinformation and propaganda against Ukraine for months. Reports uncovered websites that mimicked media outlets like Bild and Spiegel. But it doesn’t stop there. Research by CORRECTIV.Faktencheck shows that the Russian campaign also entails forged government documents.
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Grand Theft Europe
content: Fraudsters are every year robbing Europe’s citizens of €50 billion in tax money. A Europe-wide investigation by 63 journalists from 30 countries, coordinated by CORRECTIV.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: “With a few simple steps“: How RT DE circumvents the EU sanctions
content: In March the EU banned Russian broadcasters. Among them RT, formerly known as Russia Today. Since then, its German version RT DE has been advertising digital loopholes to work around its suspension. Above all: mirror pages, dozens of them. Why have the responsible German authorities not managed to shut them down?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: The Heartland Lobby
content: An undercover investigation from CORRECTIV reveals how the American Heartland Institute is supporting climate change deniers in Germany with the goal of undermining climate protection measures.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: The President and the Arms Dealer
content: A story of international arms deals – and expensive gifts in Berlin. A CORRECTIV investigation with public broadcaster ZDF’s Frontal21 and Stern magazine.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Flight MH17
content:
It’s one of the greatest war crimes of modern times – and the truth still hasn’t been told. On July 17th 2014 at about 16:20 local time, Malaysia Airlines flight MH17 was shot down over eastern Ukraine. All 298 passengers died, including many children. Who fired the missile? Over several months the Germany-based non-profit investigative […]

The post Flight MH17 appeared first on correctiv.org.
creator: Anne-Lise Bouyer

title: “The fight is the same”: How “Unser Mitteleuropa” is building a network of right-wing media in Europe
content: They stir up hatred against migration or spread misinformation: Media outlets from eleven European countries are cooperating to increase their influence. They are close to parties like the FPÖ, the AfD or Fratelli d’Italia. At the heart of it all: the website “Unser Mitteleuropa” (Our Central Europe) and a dead Austrian man with contacts to the right-wing extremist scene.
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: The Gazprom-Lobby
content: How Russia enlisted German politicians, business leaders and lawyers to ensure German dependence on Russian gas.
creator: Belén Ríos Falcón

title: Monitoring Disinformation around Elections: Key Takeaways from the German Federal Elections in 2021
content: This report details the knowledge that CORRECTIV.Faktencheck has gained over five years of monitoring mis- and disinformation during elections. It highlights the 2021 federal elections in Germany and the key learnings that can be drawn from it. This includes the different tasks within the team, monitored platforms, tools, and thoughts behind the chosen approach.
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Reports of sexual violence in the war: Why the Ukrainian parliament dismissed Human Rights Chief Denisova
content: The Ukrainian parliament dismissed Human Rights Chief Lyudmila Denisova amid claims that she spoke unethically about rape and sexual violence committed by Russian soldiers against Ukrainian civilians. Some German-language websites accuse her of inventing cases all together. What truth is there to these accusations?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Lawsuit Against Former Pope Benedict in Abuse Scandal
content:
Pope Emeritus Benedict XVI. may have to stand trial in a secular court over an 
abuse scandal in the Catholic Church.
creator: Justus von Daniels

title: Fake news of the week: War in Ukraine
content: Since Russia's invasion of Ukraine, German social media has been flooded with misinformation and images shared out of context.
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: BioNTech’s political meddling
content: Did Uğur Şahin, CEO of BioNTech, threaten to suspend all political donations?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: The refugee train
content: Half-empty beer bottles, rubbish on the floor, graffiti... but who vandalised this train?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: Russian sanctions
content: Last week, Twitter users claimed their accounts were being closed due to Russian sanctions - what was really going on?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: A DDR flag at the Winter Olympics
content: This week, misinformation spread online about an East German flag at the Winter Olympics.
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake News of the Week: Two million march in Ottawa
content: This week, misinformation spread online claiming to show two million people in the streets
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: Do the Greens want Sharia law?
content: Online misinformation targeted Green co-leader Omid Nouripour
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: The anti-vax helicopter pilot
content: Did this heroic rescue helicopter pilot take a principled stand against vaccination? (he didn't)
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: Fake news of the week: Annalena Baerbock’s secret weapon
content: What was Putin really laughing about?
creator: Sophie Timmermann

title: noFake: an interdisciplinary project, combatting disinformation with human and artificial intelligence
content: In the fight against disinformation, the nonprofit investigative journalism newsroom CORRECTIV and scholars from Ruhr University Bochum (RUB) and Technical University (TU) Dortmund team up for the project noFake. By using crowdsourcing and artificial intelligence (AI), noFake intents to identify and refute false reports.
creator: Marc Steinau

title: The European network of corona-deniers
content: Since the beginning of the pandemic, corona-deniers have been trying to build an international network. But how well connected are they really ?
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Barriers to abortion in Germany
content: Humiliation, lack of information and denied aftercare: Hundreds share their experiences getting an abortion in Germany
creator: Belén Ríos Falcón

title: Fake news of the week: The Berlin power cut
content: On January 9, Berlin experienced a significant power cut. Soon afterwards, misinformation spread online about its cause
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Sanctions Tracker – Live monitoring of all sanctions against Russia
content: Sanctions are the main weapon of the West in the Ukraine conflict. But their effectiveness remains an open question. We’re providing daily updates on which embargoes are imposed on whom and answering your most important questions.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: CumEx Files 2.0 – The outrageous tax fraud goes on
content: It’s the biggest tax heist in history. Three years after publishing the first CumEx Files, an international media collaboration led by CORRECTIV exposes how taxpayers worldwide were cheated out of €150 billion. An insider reports on cross-border deals, financial experts warn of continued tax fraud and authorities refuse responsibility.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: CumEx Files 2.0: How did we calculate €150 billion in tax loss?
content: The new CumEx Files investigation reveals that the tax damage is significantly greater than previously thought: €150 billion. This is how we estimated the sum of the massive fraud.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: The CumEx-Files
content:
DEUTSCHE VERSION CumEx-Files 2.0 – The outrageous tax fraud goes on It is the largest tax robbery in the history of Europe. A group of bankers, lawyers and traders plundered at least eleven countries for years – their loot: €55bn. CORRECTIV and 18 media partners have investigated for a year to expose the inner workings […]

The post The CumEx-Files appeared first on correctiv.org.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Mutilated Women + Pharma Bullies + Queer Foster Families
content: The best of journalism – with investigations from ARTE, ProPublica, The Bureau of Investigative Journalism and many more
creator: Luise Lange-Letellier

title: CORRECTIV.Faktencheck’s Data Analysis: Facebook and Youtube Are the Most Important Platforms for False Information
content: Where do people come across false information on the Internet and how do they spread it? An evaluation of the data for 2020 from our CrowdNewsroom shows what role the big tech companies play.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: NURSES FOR SALE
content: Un negocio brutal: intermediarios alemanes usan prácticas dudosas que convierten a las enfermeras extranjeras en una mercancía
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: NURSES FOR SALE
content: An unsettling business: how agents are commoditizing foreign nurses for German healthcare
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: No Filter for the Right
content: How Right-Wing recruiters use Instagram to attract young people
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Data analysis: users find questionable information on the coronavirus especially on Youtube and disseminate it via Whatsapp
content: CORRECTIV.Faktencheck has evaluated more than 1,800 reader submissions on possible disinformation about the coronavirus. The analysis shows that Whatsapp is the most common dissemination channel for questionable information – and Youtube is the place where it can be found. What are social media companies doing about it?
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: EU Banking Authority on Cum-Ex Trades: No coordination between national supervisors
content: The CumEx Files, an investigation led by CORRECTIV, revealed €55bn in international tax fraud. After publication, the EU Parliament demanded that the European Banking Authority investigate how the so-called dividend arbitrage trades had been possible and what supervisors and regulators had done to prevent them. Now the EBA has released its report and action plan.
creator: Justus von Daniels

title: Datenanalyse: Nutzer finden fragwürdige Corona-Informationen vor allem auf Youtube und verbreiten sie über Whatsapp
content: CORRECTIV.Faktencheck hat mehr als 1.800 Hinweise von Leserinnen und Lesern zu möglicher Desinformation über das Coronavirus ausgewertet. Die Analyse zeigt: Whatsapp ist der häufigste Verbreitungskanal für fragwürdige Informationen – und Youtube ist der Ort, an dem sie zu finden sind. Was tun die Konzerne dagegen?
creator: Alice Echtermann

title: A Kick On Pills – Painkillers in Football
content: The abuse of painkillers is a major issue in football. A year-long investigation. Interviews with over 150 people, including players, doctors and experts. A survey with more than 1,100 participants from German amateur football leagues. The #KickonPills investigation by CORRECTIV and the ARD Doping Editorial Team sheds light on a system that sidelines health for wealth.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: How is coronavirus impacting you? We want to listen.
content: With our CrowdNewsroom we provide a platform for you to share experiences and tips. You can help us report how the COVID-19 crisis affects your job.
creator: Justus von Daniels

title: Wanted: Influencer, Young, Right-wing
content: The New Right is putting a lot of effort into building up a network of media outlets and influencers, very often used to spread disinformation. Young YouTubers like Niklas Lotz, with his channel “Neverforgetniki”, or Naomi Seibt benefit from this. We reveal who lent a hand in their ascent.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Ratzinger and the Pedophile Priest
content: A priest convicted of sexually abusing children says that, on a winter day, Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger is standing on his doorstep. Now, an investigation conducted by CORRECTIV and Frontal21 reveals the ties of Pope Emeritus Benedict XVI with the priest. The stories shared by alleged victims who came forward during the investigation show how the Catholic Church’s prosecution of sexual abuse within its own ranks is insufficient.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Grand Theft Europe – The News Game
content: Dive into the world of VAT fraudsters
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: How the UK is combating VAT fraud
content: When it comes to combating VAT carousel fraud, the UK has been the EU’s most successful member. Rod Stone, a former tax investigator, explains.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: CORRECTIV publishes graphic novel on Congo massacres
content: The graphic novel is another example of our efforts to combine journalism and art.
creator: Frederik Richter

title: Frontex: watching the watchers
content: Shortly before the European elections, the EU rushed through a reform of the border agency Frontex. The agency is becoming more powerful than ever. Hardly anyone can control whether the agency respects basic rights of refugees. Whether or not it really tries to save lives at sea. Nonetheless, transparency activists try their best – in their very own way. A joint investigation with the ARD magazine Report Munich.
creator: marius.wolf@correctiv.org

title: Black Sites Turkey
content: In a near-repeat of the CIA’s ‘extraordinary renditions’, the regime of Turkish president Erdoğan is kidnapping dozens of members of the Gülen movement from around the world. Victims are now raising a serious accusation: secret torture sites are part of the repression. A team of nine media organizations from eight countries, coordinated by CORRECTIV, investigates.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: The CumEx Files
content: How Europe's taxpayers have been swindled of €55 billion.
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: Trailer #CumExFiles
content: The vast so-called cum ex tax scandal which has rocked Germany in the past decade has already cost the country an estimated €30 billion. It was assumed that a change in the law in 2016 definitively outlawed such trades. But as a cross-border and undercover investigation now reveals, the trade is still flourishing and has targeted far more countries and has cost far more than was previously thought, affecting nearly all of the biggest economies in Europe. Is this the heist of the century?
creator: Benjamin Schubert

title: In the hands of the mafia
content: Italy is a leading exporter of tomatoes, raking in billions of euros every year. The workers in the fields earn very little. Sexual abuse of female workers is widespread. Compared to Spain and Morocco, their situation is worse as the mafia is holding a tight grip on the region’s labour.
creator: Pascale Müller

title: The dark side of Morocco’s booming agricultural exports
content: Just like in Spain, female workers on Morocco’s fruit and vegetable fields are subject to wide-spread sexual abuse. But in one aspect, their situation is different.
creator: Pascale Müller

title: Rape in the fields
content: Harassed, insulted and raped – that is everyday for thousands of women working in tomato and strawberry fields of Spain, Italy and Morocco. The vegetables and fruits they harvest are sold in German supermarkets and all over Europe. First part of our investigation of workers’ sexual abuse in Spain, Morocco and Italy.
creator: Pascale Müller

title: Oliver Schröm new editor-in-chief at CORRECTIV
content:
We are pleased to announce Oliver Schröm will join CORRECTIV as the new editor-in-chief. In 2012, Schröm and senior reporter David Schraven developed the concept for and subsequently founded CORRECTIV. For various reasons, Oliver could not join the initial CORRECTIV team. However, he has always been involved in our project including most recently as chairman […]

The post Oliver Schröm new editor-in-chief at CORRECTIV appeared first on correctiv.org.
creator: David Schraven

title: The Girl in Abidjan
content: Western Africa is an important hub for cocaine shipments from Latin America to Europe. The Ivory Coast could be a new knot in the smuggling network of the Italian ‘ndrangheta. The country is home to a mafia boss’ daughter who took refuge in the country after a doomed love affair forced her to leave Italy. But once you’re family, you’re never out of the reach of the ‘ndrangheta, as this investigation shows.
creator: Giulio Rubino

title: CORRECTIV’s Nerds Team is hiring a developer
content:
Are you a Python full-stack developer? Join us!

The post CORRECTIV’s Nerds Team is hiring a developer appeared first on correctiv.org.
creator: Simon Wörpel

title: French seaside resort heading under the waves
content: Palavas-les-Flots is a popular seaside resort in southern France, one of several built in the 1960s as part of a government programme to develop tourism along the western Mediterranean seaboard. But the town, like others in the region, now faces disaster from the slow but sure rising sea levels and the coastal erosion exacerbated by mass tourism.
creator: Jade Lindgaard
